title: Nichrome Hot Wire Foam Cutter Drive
design_question: How to heat a cutting wire to the correct temperature for clean EPS/EPP foam cutting without burning?
selected_approach: 28 AWG Nichrome 80/20 wire (R ≈ 2.15 Ω/m) tensioned between two 3D-printed PETG end brackets with a stainless-steel spring at one end. 12 V / 3 A bench supply (or MOSFET PWM at 10 kHz from 12 V) gives 5.6–8 W dissipation depending on wire length, reaching 280–350 °C — ideal for 3 mm/s cutting speed in 50 mm EPS foam.
rationale: Nichrome is the standard resistance heating alloy for this application: stable resistance, oxidation-resistant to 1200 °C, inexpensive. Spring tensioning compensates thermal expansion (wire lengthens ~0.3 mm per metre per 100 °C increase). PWM power control via N-MOSFET enables temperature regulation without a variable power supply.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Higher resistivity suitable for 230 V applications; unnecessarily high R for 12 V low-power cutters.
Complex, expensive, safety enclosure required; overkill for low-volume foam shaping.

verification_notes: FACT: 'Nichrome Resistance Heating Wire Power Calculation' confirms P = V²/R formula and recommended 200–400 °C operating range for foam cutting.
supporting_fact_titles: Nichrome Resistance Heating Wire Power Calculation
N-Channel MOSFET as a Low-Side Power Switch
